Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Re Special Motion To Strike Third And Fourth Causes Of Action In Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Eric Wafer (415.307.0057) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Christine Long, Esq. (408.286.5800; christine.long@berliner.com) of Berliner Cohen LLP for Defendant, Kevin Glenn.

Ms. Long stated there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KEVIN GLENN'S Motion For Attorneys Fees Re Special Motion To Strike Third And Fourth Causes Of Action In Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint:

Kevin Glenn's effectively unopposed "motion for attorney's fees re special motion to strike third and fourth causes of action in plaintiff's first amended complaint" is granted.

Plaintiff filed three documents on August 1, 2022, none of which substantively responded to Glenn's fees motion. If plaintiff's papers are read as a motion to reconsider this court's May 2022 order granting Green's anti-SLAPP motion, plaintiff's motion is much belated and does not meet the requirements of CCP 1008.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion To Amend 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Eric Wafer (415.307.0057) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Christine Long, Esq. (408.286.5800; christine.long@berliner.com) of Berliner Cohen LLP for Defendant, Kevin Glenn.

Ms. Long stated there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ERIC WAFER's Motion To Amend 1st Amended Complaint:

"Plaintiff's motion to amend 1st amended complaint" is denied. First, plaintiff fails to meet the requirements for a motion to amend. (See, e.g, CRC 3.1324.) Second, amendment of a complaint is not permitted after the court, as here, finds it subject to the anti-SLAPP statute. (Simmons v. Allstate Insurance Co. (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 1068, 1073-74.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
